problem.title: Edge Window Sum
problem.scenario: A telemetry review service receives ordered integer readings from a device window after an upload completes. Analysts use a small edge-window check to compare the first and final values before running deeper diagnostics.
problem.task: Given n readings, return the sum of the first and last reading. If the window contains one reading, return that value once.
problem.description: Given n integers, print the sum of the first and last value. If there is only one value, print that value once.
problem.inputFormat: The first line contains n. The second line contains n space-separated integers.
problem.outputFormat: Print one integer: the edge window sum.

examples.explanation: The first value is 4 and the last value is 7.
A single value is counted once.

starterCode.python:
import sys

def main():
    tokens = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    n = tokens[0]
    values = tokens[1:1 + n]
    print(edge_sum(values))

def edge_sum(values: list[int]) -> int:
    # TODO: return the sum of the first and last value
    return 0

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

referenceSolutions.python:
import sys

tokens = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
n = tokens[0]
values = tokens[1:1 + n]
print(values[0] if n == 1 else values[0] + values[-1])

generationMetadata.promptText: Create an easy arrays problem about edge values.
generationMetadata.intendedTechnique: Read the first and last values while handling n = 1.
